title: Well done, Ben and Lucy!
big_question: Hỏi về sở thích và những trải nghiệm cuối tuần bằng tiếng Anh thì nói như thế nào nhỉ?
grammar: Present simple questions: Do you like (reading books)? / What do you do in your free time?
Past simple questions: What did you do at the weekend? / Did you win the match?
skills: Asking and answering personal questions
Talking about town events and fairground rides
speaking_prompts: What's your favourite fairground ride?
What did you do at the weekend?
Do you want to be famous?

mini_games.prompt: This person takes pictures with a camera at a special event.
You drive these small cars at the funfair and bump into your friends.
What _______ you do at the weekend?

title: In the museum
big_question: Làm sao để nói về nội quy (phải làm/không được làm) và các đồ vật thời xưa nhỉ?
grammar: Obligation and prohibition: I/You must (wear a helmet) - I/You mustn't (run or shout).
Direct and indirect objects: Give me / him / her / us / them (the sword), please.
phonics: The letter sound 'ow' (e.g., crown, bow, town)
skills: Talking about knights, queens and historical items
Expressing rules and obligations
Reading and listening for specific information
project: Make a timeline about things in your house
speaking_prompts: How can we learn about the past?
Give me the bracelet, please.
What must or mustn't you do at the museum?

mini_games.prompt: A king or queen wears this beautiful, shiny object on their head.
A knight holds this in his hand to protect himself in a fight.
You _______ touch the exhibits in the museum. It's not allowed!

title: The world around us
big_question: Chúng ta biết gì về thế giới bên ngoài nhỉ?
grammar: Connectors: but, and, because, so (e.g., I was tired, so I went home.)
Past ability: I could / couldn't (run 20 kilometres).
phonics: Silent consonants (e.g., island, rhino, write, sign, listen)
skills: Reading about maps and satellite images
Listening and writing about the countryside and free time
project: Label a map with five favourite places
speaking_prompts: What do we know about the outside world?
What could or couldn't you do when you were younger?
Where is your favourite outdoor place?

mini_games.prompt: A piece of land with water all around it.
A very high hill, often with snow on top.
I didn't swim _______ the water was really cold!

title: Danger!
big_question: Nước có thể gây ra những nguy hiểm gì nhỉ?
grammar: Past continuous: I was (climbing a tree). We were (playing tennis).
Past continuous questions: What was she doing? / Was he playing?
phonics: The sound /aɪ/ with different spellings (e.g., bike, light, night, fly)
skills: Reading and listening about emergencies
Talking about background actions in the past
speaking_prompts: What were you doing yesterday at 5 o'clock?
How can water be dangerous?
What should you do when there is a flood or a fire?

mini_games.prompt: A special van or car that takes sick or injured people to the hospital.
This person wears a uniform and puts out fires.
What _______ you doing yesterday afternoon?

title: Two return tickets
big_question: Đi mua vé tàu như thế nào nhỉ?
grammar: Prepositions of time: in (September/the morning), at (eight o'clock), on (Sunday).
Past continuous and past simple: I was (having dinner) when you phoned me.
phonics: The sound /eə/ (e.g., stairs, square, bear, hair)
skills: Reading about travel and stories
Listening and speaking at the train station
project: Make an advertisement for one type of transport
speaking_prompts: What's the best way to travel?
Can I have a return ticket to London, please?
What were you doing when the phone rang?

mini_games.prompt: You carry your things in it. You carry it on your back.
It takes you up or down to another floor without walking.
I always do my homework _______ the evening.

title: Police!
big_question: Làm thế nào để miêu tả ngoại hình của mọi người nhỉ?
grammar: Past habits: He used to (be a police officer).
Past obligation: We had to (be really careful) / I had to tidy up.
phonics: The sounds /ɑː/ and /ɔː/ (e.g., dark, scar, short, tall)
skills: Reading about a robbery
Writing and listening about people and habits
project: Make a sketch of a friend and write a description
speaking_prompts: How can we describe people?
What did your dad use to look like?
What did you have to do when you were young?

mini_games.prompt: Hair that grows on a man's chin and cheeks.
Hair that is not straight but has a lot of ringlets.
When I was young, I _______ to cry all the time!

title: Mythical beasts
big_question: Các loài động vật thời xưa trông như thế nào nhỉ?
grammar: Comparatives and superlatives: longer / more dangerous than, better / worse than, the biggest / heaviest / most dangerous, the best / worst.
Questions with look like: What does (a unicorn) look like? - It looks like (a horse), but it's got (a big horn).
phonics: The letter sound 'ea' (e.g., feathers, meat, heavy)
skills: Listening for specific information
Speaking and writing about imaginary beasts
speaking_prompts: What were animals like long ago?
What does a unicorn look like?
Which is the most dangerous animal in the world?

mini_games.prompt: Birds have these on their bodies to keep them warm and help them fly.
A hard, pointed part that grows on the head of some animals like a rhino.
The blue whale is the _______ animal in the world.

title: Orchestra practice
big_question: Các loại nhạc cụ khác nhau như thế nào nhỉ?
grammar: Possessive pronouns: mine, yours, his, hers, ours, theirs.
Relative pronouns (who, which, where): Connor's the boy who plays the recorder. / The house where Mark lives is green.
phonics: The sounds /ɜː/ and /ɔː/ (e.g., bird, work, floor, door)
skills: Reading about music and feelings
Writing and listening for detail
project: Make a Carroll diagram with types of musical instruments
speaking_prompts: How are musical instruments different?
Is this guitar yours or his?
What is your favourite band or singer?

mini_games.prompt: A brass musical instrument that you blow into, which has three buttons.
A stringed instrument that you hold under your chin and play with a bow.
This book isn't my book. It is _______ (she).

title: In the planetarium
big_question: Tại sao vũ trụ lại thú vị nhỉ?
grammar: Predictions with will: What will you be when you grow up? - I'll be a (police officer).
Adverbs of manner: angrily, badly, beautifully, carefully, dangerously, happily, loudly, quickly, quietly, slowly.
phonics: Word stress and the weak sound /ə/ (schwa)
skills: Listening and reading about space
Speaking and writing about the future
project: Write questions to ask an astronaut
speaking_prompts: Why is space exciting?
What will you be when you grow up?
What can you see through the telescope?

mini_games.prompt: You use this instrument to look at the stars and planets.
A person who is trained to travel and work in space.
The spaceship travelled through space _______ (quiet).

title: At the campsite
big_question: Chúng ta biết gì về cắm trại nhỉ?
grammar: Expressions of quantity: some cheese, a loaf / two loaves of bread, a piece of cheese, a packet of crisps, a bottle of milk, a can of lemonade.
Questions about quantity: How much (cheese) do we need? / How many (bottles of water) do we need?
phonics: Unstressed words in phrases (e.g., a, of, the, and)
skills: Reading, listening and speaking about camping
Talking about measurements and holiday plans
clil_topic: Maths: Measurements (how much, how long, how wide, how high, how heavy)
project: Make an advertisement for something to take on a camping holiday
speaking_prompts: What do we know about camping?
How much water have we got?
How many packets of crisps do we need?

mini_games.prompt: You do this with wood to keep warm and cook food at the campsite.
You sleep inside this when you go camping in the forest.
How _______ water do we need for the trip?
